name: Snowflake Inc.
shortDescription: Snowflake operates the AI Data Cloud, a fully managed, multi-cloud platform that unifies data warehousing, data lakes, data engineering, and AI workloads across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
businessModel: Snowflake generates revenue almost entirely through a consumption-based model — customers pay for compute credits and storage used rather than fixed seats or licenses, which creates a natural land-and-expand dynamic. FY2026 product revenue reached $4.72 billion (up ~29% YoY), with a net revenue retention rate of ~124%, reflecting strong upsell within the installed base. A small professional services segment (~$212M) rounds out total revenue. Customer segments skew heavily enterprise, with 779 customers generating over $1M in trailing-12-month product revenue.
marketPositioning: Snowflake positions itself as the neutral, cloud-agnostic data layer sitting above the hyperscalers — offering capabilities AWS Redshift, Google BigQuery, and Azure Fabric intentionally restrict to lock customers into their own clouds. Against open-source-centric Databricks, Snowflake competes on enterprise governance, ease of administration, and a unified SQL-first interface, while expanding into AI/ML workloads via Cortex AI and Snowpark to close the gap on data science use cases.
growthDrivers: AI and ML workload expansion via Cortex AI and Snowpark, driving new compute consumption
Data sharing and monetization through the Snowflake Marketplace, creating network effects across the Data Cloud ecosystem
International expansion across 49 multi-cloud regional deployments on AWS, Azure, and GCP
Migration of on-premises data warehouses (Teradata, Oracle) to cloud-native architecture
Upsell of new products (Iceberg Tables, Snowflake Native Apps, Streamlit) to existing customers
techDifferentiation: Snowflake's proprietary three-layer architecture — separating storage, compute, and cloud services so each scales independently — was built natively for the cloud and is difficult for legacy vendors to replicate without a ground-up rebuild. Its multi-cloud abstraction layer (running across AWS, Azure, and GCP simultaneously) allows customers to share and query data across clouds without copying it, a capability the hyperscalers structurally cannot offer without undermining their own lock-in strategies.
